metadata.description: Make AI agents dramatically cheaper at running your infrastructure. Drop-in Claude Code plugins that collapse slow, multi-step API dances into one fast call. Zero install pain: single-file Python, also runs standalone. Universal primitives only — service wrappers (github-remote, vercel-remote, supabase-remote, railway-ops, etc.) live in osouthgate/agent-plus-skills.

plugins.name: agent-plus-meta
plugins.description: The meta plugin for the agent-plus framework. Workspace bootstrap, env-var readiness, identity cache, marketplace lifecycle (init/install/list/update/remove/search/prefer), extensions. One call to discover what's installed, what's configured, and what services this checkout already knows about. Names only, no values.

plugins.name: repo-analyze
plugins.description: One-call cold-start orientation for an unfamiliar repo — file tree (capped), language mix, framework + build-tool detection, top-level deps, entrypoints, README highlights. Replaces ~67-grep + ~60-ls cold-start dance with one structured JSON blob. Stdlib only, no network, stateless.

plugins.name: diff-summary
plugins.description: One-call structured triage of a git diff — per-file role classification (source/test/config/doc/generated/build/fixture/migration), risk tier with reasons, public-API touch detection, co-changed-test detection, secret-risk path flagging. Replaces the 5–20 Read calls an agent burns reading each modified file to figure out 'what kind of change is this'. Stdlib only, no network, stateless.

plugins.name: skill-feedback
plugins.description: Local-first agent self-assessment for Claude Code skills. Agent appends a structured rating + friction note to .agent-plus/skill-feedback/<skill>.jsonl after each skill use. Skill authors aggregate locally with `report` or bundle entries into a GitHub issue body for the skill's source repo with `submit`. No SaaS, no SDK, scrub-on-write.

plugins.name: skill-plus
plugins.description: Mine the Claude Code session log to find commands you keep typing by hand, scaffold them into project-scoped Claude skills under .claude/skills/<name>/, audit existing skills against the framework contract, close the loop with a cross-source feedback aggregator that joins skill-feedback ratings against implicit session-mining failure signals, and promote skills you keep using to your <user>/agent-plus-skills marketplace. Stdlib Python, local-only, no network. Consent-gated and secret-redacting before disk write.
